nav.a11yReadPageEmpty: No readable text in this area.
nav.handNavHelp: Finger navigation: point with your index finger in front of the camera. The cursor follows your finger. Hold 0.8 s on an element to click. Without this feature, use Tab to move between controls and Enter or Space to activate buttons.

emailPage.subject: Subject
emailPage.emptyTrashConfirm: Permanently delete all messages in Trash?
emailPage.composeDraftHint: Save your changes anytime. To send, choose a recipient and fill subject and message.
emailPage.sendNeedsSubjectBody: Subject and message are required to send.

patientDashboard.alertFlaggedBody: Your vitals require follow-up — your care team has been notified.
patientDashboard.wellbeingEmpty: Complete today's check-in to see your wellbeing summary.
patientDashboard.chartNoData: No data yet — complete a check-in!
patientDashboard.chartEmptyHint: No data yet — log your vitals (you can add several times per day) to see trends here.
patientDashboard.riskHigh: High risk — please contact your care team.
patientDashboard.riskScoreEmpty: Complete today's check-in to calculate your risk score.
patientDashboard.historyEmpty: No history yet. Logs will appear here after your first check-in.

patient.title: Clinical messaging — CHU Abdelhamid Ben Badis
patient.lead: Exchange with your care team (nurse, doctor). If your vitals trigger an alert, the team may contact you here for verification.
patient.bullet1: Messages complement clinical follow-up; for life-threatening emergencies, call emergency services (15).
patient.bullet2: A trace of exchanges for your hospital record.

nurse.title: Messaging — First responder
nurse.lead: Channel for quick verification after a report (history, patient contact, triage). Aligned with the “digital alert → action” flow at the hospital.
nurse.bullet1: Check trend vs isolated spike, contact the patient, summarize for the physician.
nurse.bullet2: Escalate to the physician if risk is confirmed.

doctor.title: Clinical messaging — Decision & closure
doctor.lead: Therapeutic instructions, referral to consultation or emergency, coordination with the nurse. An alert is only closed after documented human action (acknowledgement / closure note in the care pathway).
doctor.bullet1: Instruction, consultation request or emergency referral according to clinical profile.
doctor.bullet2: Traceability for hospital audit (response within safety timeframes).

sidebar.adminAlertsSupervisionTooltip: Supervision of alerts and notifications — open the center to review and filter.

adminWorkspace.alertsSupervisionDesc: Review platform alerts, appointment signals, and staff notifications. Use the header bell for quick access.

adminDashboard.pageLead: Live overview of MediFollow (data from your database).
adminDashboard.occ_pendingShare: Share of pending requests (of active appointments)
adminDashboard.occ_nursingStaffShare: Nursing share of clinical staff (nurses / doctors + nurses)

doctorMyPatients.subtitle: Patients for whom you are the attending physician.
doctorMyPatients.empty: No patients are assigned to you yet.

doctorPatientDossier.notFoundOrUnassigned: Patient not found or not assigned to your care.
doctorPatientDossier.loadDossierError: Unable to load the patient record.
doctorPatientDossier.sosModalLead: The patient has shared a critical location. Open the map to respond without delay.
doctorPatientDossier.noVitalsYet: No vital readings for this patient.
doctorPatientDossier.lastReadingOk: Last reading with no automatic threshold alerts.
doctorPatientDossier.lastCheckinNoVitals: Last check-in without detailed vital measurements.
doctorPatientDossier.alertRowNoVitals: No vital measurements on this reading.
doctorPatientDossier.vitalClosureLead: Write the solution or instructions for the patient: they will be sent in secure messaging when you resolve. Each row = one distinct reading; "Resolve all" sends the same note for each reading listed below.
doctorPatientDossier.bulkNotePlaceholder: e.g. continue paracetamol as prescribed, monitor temperature, call back if fever above 38.5 °C…
doctorPatientDossier.noteBeforeResolve: Write an instruction for the patient before resolving.
doctorPatientDossier.noteBeforeResolveAll: Write an instruction for the patient before resolving all.
doctorPatientDossier.instructionPlaceholder: Your plan: treatment, monitoring, when to seek emergency care…
doctorPatientDossier.noTreatments: No treatment recorded for this patient.
doctorPatientDossier.intakeLead: Days the patient indicated intake — up to 5 recent days per treatment.
doctorPatientDossier.noOtherReadings: No other readings in this period.
doctorPatientDossier.protocolIntro1: Assign the department's standard protocol (milestones J+3, J+7, …) from the discharge date.
doctorPatientDossier.protocolIntro2: Add a supplemental questionnaire if needed (e.g. comorbidity).
doctorPatientDossier.alertGlobalRisk: Global risk score: {{score}}/100 — clinical vigilance recommended.

doctorNurseEscalations.pageLead: History of cases where a nurse requested your input (patients for whom you are the attending physician). Use the + button for the patient instruction (closure), an urgent appointment, or a new medication via the dedicated dialogs. Statuses: pending or resolved.
doctorNurseEscalations.emptyNoEscalations: No nurse escalations recorded for your patients.
doctorNurseEscalations.modalInstructionLead: This message is sent to the patient in secure messaging and closes this alert.
doctorNurseEscalations.modalRdvPatientLead: Patient: {{name}}. The appointment is created as confirmed.
doctorNurseEscalations.medModalLead: Patient: {{name}}. The treatment will appear on the patient dashboard.
doctorNurseEscalations.alertNoteRequired: Write an instruction for the patient before closing.

doctorDepartmentNurses.subtitleNoDept: Your profile does not yet have a department assigned (by an administrator).
doctorDepartmentNurses.emptyNoDept: No department on your account — the list of nurses in the same service cannot be displayed.
doctorDepartmentNurses.emptyNoNurses: No nurses registered for this department.

doctorDepartmentDoctors.subtitleNoDept: Your profile does not yet have a department assigned (by an administrator).
doctorDepartmentDoctors.emptyNoDept: No department on your account — the list of doctors in the same service cannot be displayed.
doctorDepartmentDoctors.emptyNoDoctors: No doctors registered for this department.

doctorPrescriptions.loginHint: Sign in as a doctor to manage prescriptions.
doctorPrescriptions.pageSubtitle: Add prescribed medications for each patient you follow. They appear on the patient dashboard.
doctorPrescriptions.noPatientsAssigned: No patients are assigned to you. An administrator must assign patients to your care team.
doctorPrescriptions.selectPatientFirst: Choose a patient on the left first.
doctorPrescriptions.emptyLinesHint: Lines without a medication name are ignored when saving.
doctorPrescriptions.errorAtLeastOneMedication: Enter at least one medication name.

doctorAvailabilityCalendar.title: Availability calendar
doctorAvailabilityCalendar.intro: Appointments confirmed by administration appear automatically on each day. Click a day to edit your availability slots (from … to …), split into 30-minute slots for new requests.
doctorAvailabilityCalendar.saveErrorAuthHint: {{msg}} If you are also logged in as a patient, sign out the patient or use a private window for the doctor account.
doctorAvailabilityCalendar.needAtLeastOneSlot: Add at least one time range on a day (click a day on the calendar) then save.
doctorAvailabilityCalendar.modalHelp: Enter one or more continuous ranges (e.g. 09:00 → 12:00). The end is exclusive: the last slot starts 30 minutes before the end time.
doctorAvailabilityCalendar.noSlotsClosed: No slots — this day is closed.

editDoctor.passwordMinLength: Password must be at least 6 characters.

editNurse.passwordMinLength: Password must be at least 6 characters.

privacySetting.accountPrivacyDesc: When enabled, your profile is only visible to your care team and authorized staff.
privacySetting.activityDesc: Allow your care team to see when you were last active in the application.
privacySetting.storyDesc: Allow sharing relevant health updates with your care team when appropriate.
privacySetting.photoDesc: Choose how photos are added or tagged on your profile.
privacySetting.profileDesc: Control who can see your profile information.
privacySetting.loginNotifDesc: Receive alerts when your account is accessed from a new device.

nurseDashboard.loginForAlerts: Sign in to see urgent alerts from your patients.
nurseDashboard.noOpenAlerts: No open vital-signs alerts for your assigned patients.
nurseDashboard.reminderHint: Reminder: reply to the patient in secure messaging; if you have clinical concerns, escalate to the referring physician — they will receive a notification and a message on the patient–doctor thread.
nurseDashboard.modalEscalatePlaceholder: e.g. patient still short of breath despite advice, persistent high BP…
nurseDashboard.cardOpenVitalsSub: Urgent readings not yet closed by the doctor
nurseDashboard.roleBullet1: Daily follow-up: monitoring patients in post-hospitalization follow-up.
nurseDashboard.roleBullet2: Data entry: recording health data and observations.
nurseDashboard.roleBullet3: Alert tracking: managing notifications and patient alerts.

careCoordinatorDashboard.quickNotificationsDesc: Alerts and updates about patient follow-up.
careCoordinatorDashboard.quickChatDesc: Chat with care teams and patients.
careCoordinatorDashboard.introHint: Your space for care coordination. Use the shortcuts above to stay informed and connected with teams.
careCoordinatorDashboard.quickPatientsDesc: Patients in your department with a follow-up score (vitals and medications).
careCoordinatorDashboard.quickAppointmentsDesc: All appointments for patients in your department.
careCoordinatorDashboard.quickCommunicationDesc: Message templates and department contacts for chat.
careCoordinatorDashboard.statFollowUpHint: Patients with overall score below 50% over the last {{days}} days

careCoordinatorCommunication.intro: Reusable message texts for follow-ups and quick access to useful department contacts (internal messaging).
careCoordinatorCommunication.templatesHint: Copy the text to paste into chat or email. Adjust for context and the patient.
careCoordinatorCommunication.contactsHint: Open a direct conversation with a physician or nurse in the same department.

template_vitals.title: Vitals reminder
template_vitals.body:
Hello,

Please remember to record your vitals (blood pressure, weight, blood glucose) in the app today. Thank you.

Kind regards

template_appointment.title: Appointment reminder
template_appointment.body:
Hello,

This is a reminder of your appointment on [DATE] at [TIME]. Please bring a valid ID.

Kind regards

template_missedAppointment.title: Follow-up after missed appointment
template_missedAppointment.body:
Hello,

We are contacting you following a missed appointment. Please call us to reschedule or if you have any questions.

Kind regards

template_lab.title: Lab results
template_lab.body:
Hello,

Your lab results are available. Your physician can discuss them at your next visit.

Kind regards

template_general.title: General follow-up
template_general.body:
Hello,

We are reaching out regarding your care. Please reply if you have any questions.

Kind regards

superAdminDashboard.subtitle: Global management of the MediFollow platform.
superAdminDashboard.platformOverviewSubtitle: Live signals from care activity, appointments, and audit.
superAdminDashboard.emptyAuditNotable: No flagged entries in recent activity.

platformUsers.subtitle: Create and manage accounts only the super administrator controls: hospital admins, auditors, and care coordinators.
platformUsers.cardAdminDesc: Create an admin for a department. Credentials can be sent by email (if SMTP is configured). Assignment is synced with the department catalog.
platformUsers.cardAuditorsDesc: Quality and audit access. List, add, and edit auditor accounts.
platformUsers.cardCoordinatorsDesc: Coordinators for patient follow-up. List, add, and edit coordinator accounts.

addPlatformAdmin.sideHint: This account manages patients, doctors, and nurses within the selected department.
addPlatformAdmin.departmentHelp: Departments are loaded from the database: catalog entries plus any department or service name used by patients, doctors, nurses, or staff. You cannot assign another administrator if the catalog already links one to that name.
addPlatformAdmin.departmentEmptyOption: No departments found yet — add catalog departments or register users with a department.
addPlatformAdmin.departmentLoadError: Could not load departments. Refresh the page or try again later.
addPlatformAdmin.passwordMin: Password must be at least 6 characters.
addPlatformAdmin.emailSent: Login details were sent by email.
addPlatformAdmin.emailNotSent: Email could not be sent (check SMTP). Share credentials securely with the user.

auditorA11y.readDashboardPart2: Welcome to the MediFollow audit dashboard.
auditorA11y.readDashboardPart3: Summary cards show logs today, active users in the last twenty-four hours, total actions in thirty days, and suspicious actions.
auditorA11y.readDashboardPart4: The line chart shows activity over time. The doughnut chart shows how actions are spread across categories.
auditorA11y.readDashboardPart5: The recent logs table lists timestamp, user, role, action, category, status code, and severity.
auditorA11y.readDashboardPart6: Use the full audit logs link to open the detailed log list with filters.
auditorA11y.readDashboardPart7: Use the toolbar above for large text or to hear this page read aloud.
auditorA11y.readLogsPart2: Welcome to the MediFollow audit logs page.
auditorA11y.readLogsPart3: Filter by user email, role, action type, data type, and time period, then apply.
auditorA11y.readLogsPart4: The table lists when each action occurred, who performed it, the action type, request summary, target resource, and I P address.
auditorA11y.readLogsPart5: Open detail on a row to see snapshots before and after changes when available.
auditorA11y.readLogsPart6: Use previous and next in the pagination area when there are several pages.
auditorA11y.readLogsPart7: Use the toolbar above for large text or to hear this page read aloud.
auditorA11y.chartActivityAria: Line chart: audit activity over time
auditorA11y.chartDistributionAria: Chart: distribution of actions by category

auditorDashboard.subtitle: System-wide activity: logs, trends, and notable actions.
auditorDashboard.noChartData: Not enough data for this chart yet.

auditorLogs.subtitle: Filter and review recorded actions (create, update, delete, login…). Calls to the audit API itself are not logged.
auditorLogs.readOnlyNoSnapshots: This is a read-only request: no before/after data is stored. Use the request line above for context.
auditorLogs.legendHint: Colored rows highlight deletions and anomalies (failed login, HTTP errors).

signIn.carouselDesc: Secure access to your MediFollow space for follow-up and coordination.
signIn.intro: Enter your email and password to access your MediFollow space (doctor, patient, or nurse).
signIn.speechNotSupported: Speech recognition is not supported in your browser. Use Chrome or Edge.
signIn.speechMicDenied: Microphone not allowed. Please allow microphone access.
signIn.ttsReadError: Text-to-speech is not available in this browser.
signIn.faceStreamError: Unable to display the camera stream.
signIn.faceOpenCameraFirst: Open the camera and try again.
signIn.readPagePart2: Welcome to the MediFollow sign-in page.
signIn.readPagePart3: Step 1: enter your email, then your password.
signIn.readPagePart4: Step 2: you can use read aloud, finger navigation, and face login.
signIn.readPagePart5: Tip: use the microphone icon in the fields for manual dictation.
signIn.readPagePart6: Voice command: say stop to end dictation.
signIn.readPagePart7: Final step: click Sign in to open your session.
signIn.speechHint: Accessibility: use the microphone buttons for manual dictation. Say « stop » to stop.
signIn.handNavHelp: Finger navigation: point with your index finger in front of the camera. The cursor follows your finger. Hold 0.8 s on an element to click.
signIn.faceNotEnrolledBody: No face is registered for this account. Register your face in your profile first to use face login.

signUp.carouselDesc: Secure registration to access your MediFollow space for follow-up and coordination.

lockScreen.slide3Desc: Your data is protected and encrypted.
lockScreen.intro: Enter your credentials to access the dashboard.
lockScreen.pendingEmailCheck: A confirmation link has been sent to your email address. Click it to access your session.

adminDepartments.lead: Overview of services: see patient, physician, and nurse counts for each department.
adminDepartments.emptyHint: Create patient, doctor, or nurse accounts with a hospital department to populate this view.
adminDepartments.createCatalogTitle: Add a department to the catalog
adminDepartments.createCatalogHint: Departments you add here appear in the dropdowns when creating patient, doctor, nurse, and care coordinator accounts.
adminDepartments.createCatalogSuccess: « {{name}} » has been added and is available in account creation forms.
adminDepartments.createCatalogDuplicate: This name already exists in the catalog.
adminDepartments.deleteBody: Remove « {{name}} » from the catalog? This is only allowed when no patients, physicians, or nurses are assigned to it.

adminDepartmentDetail.lead: Accounts linked to this hospital department — filter by role or open a profile.
adminDepartmentDetail.emptyHint: Change the filter or go back to the department list.
adminDepartmentDetail.modalHint: Only doctors and nurses assigned to this department are listed.

adminAppointmentRequests.pageLead: Confirm the requested slot or propose another date and time. The patient will see the confirmed appointment in their area.
adminAppointmentRequests.messagePlaceholder: e.g. Slot proposed according to the physician’s availability.

adminQuestionnaireBank.pageLead: Create questionnaires by department, then protocols (milestones such as D+3, D+7) that reference those questionnaires. Physicians assign a protocol when the patient is discharged.
adminQuestionnaireBank.msgNeedTitle: Enter a title for the questionnaire.
adminQuestionnaireBank.msgInvalidTitle: The title must contain at least one letter (symbol-only titles such as *** are not allowed).
adminQuestionnaireBank.questionsHelp: Define each item below: label shown to the patient and answer type (scale, yes/no, text). No code required.
adminQuestionnaireBank.choicesHelp: Enter at least two lines (one option per line).
adminQuestionnaireBank.msgNeedChoiceOptions: For each multiple-choice question, add at least two options (one per line).
adminQuestionnaireBank.milestonesHelp: For each row: number of days after discharge (e.g. 3 for D+3) and the questionnaire to send, chosen from this department’s questionnaires.
adminQuestionnaireBank.noTemplatesForDept: No questionnaire saved for “{{dept}}”. Create questionnaires in the Questionnaires tab first.
adminQuestionnaireBank.msgNeedQuestion: Add at least one question with a label.
adminQuestionnaireBank.msgNeedMilestones: Add at least one milestone and choose a questionnaire for each row.
adminQuestionnaireBank.msgBadDays: Check days (D+n): use zero or positive numbers.
adminQuestionnaireBank.defaultQ1: How would you rate your recovery today?

dailyCheckIn.title: Daily Health Check-in
dailyCheckIn.lastCheckInAt: Last check-in today at {{time}} — you can add more readings below.
dailyCheckIn.flaggedAlert: Your vitals need attention. Your care team has been notified.
dailyCheckIn.emptyPrompt: Please fill in your daily health report to help your care team monitor your recovery.
dailyCheckIn.enterVitals: Fill in every field below to continue to the next step.
dailyCheckIn.allowedRanges: Allowed entry ranges: systolic BP {{sysMin}}–{{sysMax}} / diastolic {{diaMin}}–{{diaMax}} mmHg · HR {{hrMin}}–{{hrMax}} bpm · Temp {{tempMin}}–{{tempMax}} °C · SpO₂ {{o2Min}}–{{o2Max}} % · RR {{rrMin}}–{{rrMax}} /min · Weight {{wMin}}–{{wMax}} kg
dailyCheckIn.dictionaryHint: Clinical reference: temp normal 36.5–37.5 °C (critical ≥ 38.5 or < 35) · HR 60–100 (critical < 50 or > 120) · BP reference 120/80 (critical ≥ 180/120 or < 90/60) · SpO₂ ≥ 95 % (critical < 90) · RR 12–20 /min (critical > 30 or < 8) · weight: watch for rapid shifts.
dailyCheckIn.warnHrCritical: Heart rate: critical range (< 50 or > 120 bpm).
dailyCheckIn.warnBpCritical: Blood pressure: critical range (severe hypertension or hypotension).
dailyCheckIn.warnBpPartialSys: Systolic BP is very high or very low — add diastolic if you can.
dailyCheckIn.warnBpPartialDia: Diastolic BP is very high or very low — add systolic if you can.
dailyCheckIn.warnWeightRapid: Weight: change ≥ 5 % vs last entry ({{prev}} → {{next}} kg).
dailyCheckIn.symptomsIntro: Select any symptoms you are experiencing today.
dailyCheckIn.symptomsIntroStructured: Use the sliders for intensity and switches for yes/no. Free text is not used for automated analysis.
dailyCheckIn.symptomsNone: No symptoms? Great — click Next to continue.
dailyCheckIn.symptomsStructuredFooter: All sliders at zero and switches off means no notable symptoms for analysis.
dailyCheckIn.painQuestion: How is your pain level today?
dailyCheckIn.notesLabel: Additional notes for your care team (optional)
dailyCheckIn.notesPlaceholder: Describe anything unusual about how you feel today...
dailyCheckIn.errorFixVitals: Correct the out-of-range vitals before submitting.
dailyCheckIn.errorSession: Invalid session: patient ID missing. Please sign in again.
dailyCheckIn.errorSubmit: Failed to submit. Please try again.

notifications.emptyFilteredHintStaff: Change read status or type to see more.
notifications.emptyFilteredHintPatient: Try “All” for read status or change the type.

data.videoCallUnavailable: Video call unavailable for this thread

data.emptyMessages: No messages yet. Text, emoji, photo, video, document or voice below.

data.cameraPreview: Live preview — frame the shot then capture.
data.confirmRemoveBody: The conversation will be removed from your list on this device. Messages are not deleted on the server. You can restore it with “Restore hidden conversations” at the bottom of the discussion panel.

patientMedicationHistory.title: Medication history
patientMedicationHistory.intro: All your treatments (ongoing and completed): for each day a dose was checked, with the time if recorded — including the oldest dates.
patientMedicationHistory.noIntakesInPeriod: No doses recorded during this period.

patientVitalsHistory.title: Vitals history
patientVitalsHistory.intro: All your readings for the available period (several per day): vitals, how you feel, symptoms and risk score — newest first.
patientVitalsHistory.empty: No readings yet. Complete a check-in from your dashboard.
patientVitalsHistory.lastReadingSubtitle: Summary of vitals from your most recent check-in
patientVitalsHistory.noDataHr: Log your vitals to see heart rate.
patientVitalsHistory.noDataBp: Log your vitals to see blood pressure.
patientVitalsHistory.noDataO2: Log your vitals to see SpO₂.
patientVitalsHistory.noDataTemp: Log your vitals to see temperature.

labAnalysis.title: Lab report photo analysis
labAnalysis.subtitle: Upload a clear photo or scan of your lab results.
labAnalysis.whatIsPossibleAnomaly: “Possible anomaly” does not mean a specific disease was found. The tool spots words or phrases in the OCR text that often warrant review with a clinician—not a diagnosis.
labAnalysis.disclaimer: This is not medical advice. It uses OCR and keyword hints for orientation only. Always consult a qualified professional.
labAnalysis.error: Analysis failed. Check the image and try again.
labAnalysis.badType: Allowed formats: JPEG, PNG, or WebP.

anomalyStrengthBody.strong: At least one “strong” phrase was read (e.g. positive, out of range, critical). Confirm with a professional.
anomalyStrengthBody.approximate: Only “softer” hints (e.g. borderline, to monitor, anomaly without context). More uncertain—ask your clinician.

labAnalysis.detectedSignals: Phrases detected (text cues, not a diagnosis)
labAnalysis.noHints: No phrases listed—the score may rely on other text matches.

statusBody.normal: From the detected text, no strong anomaly signal was found. Still confirm with your clinician.
statusBody.anomaly: The OCR text has more “watch” cues than reassuring wording. See the list below for what triggered the flag. This is not a diagnosis.
statusBody.indeterminate: Text was too short, unreadable, or had no clear signal. Retake a sharper photo or ask your doctor.

labAnalysis.historyHint: Click an entry to see the full report.
labAnalysis.resultPlaceholder: Analysis summary will appear here after upload.
labAnalysis.referenceTableTitle: Comparison to reference intervals (adult, indicative only)
labAnalysis.intervalsDisclaimer: Ranges shown are typical adult targets for orientation only. Your lab, age, sex, pregnancy, or drugs may use different limits. Only your clinician/lab defines what applies to you.
labAnalysis.orientationsDisclaimer: These are not diagnoses. Many conditions can cause similar numbers. A doctor interprets the full picture.

doctorBrainMri.title: Brain MRI — AI-assisted review
doctorBrainMri.subtitle: Upload a brain MRI slice or export (JPEG, PNG, WebP). The system outputs a binary risk score and a Grad-CAM saliency map for documentation—not a substitute for formal radiology reporting.
doctorBrainMri.uploadPlaceholder: Select a file to preview before analysis.
doctorBrainMri.emptyResults: Run analysis to view the model classification, confidence score, and Grad-CAM overlay alongside the source image.
doctorBrainMri.probabilityCaption: Estimated probability for the positive (tumor) class. Threshold 50% for binary label.
doctorBrainMri.clinicalNote: For decision support only. Interpret in clinical context; correlation with full imaging and history is required.
doctorBrainMri.heatmapCaption: Warmer colors indicate regions that most influenced the model output.
doctorBrainMri.disclaimer: Research and decision support tool. Does not replace a licensed radiologist. Ensure the inference server is configured (trained model in brain-tumor-detection, Python/TensorFlow).
doctorBrainMri.error: Analysis failed. Check that Python/TensorFlow and the model file are available on the server.
doctorBrainMri.badType: Use a supported image type (JPEG, PNG, WebP, etc.).
doctorBrainMri.historyLoadError: Could not load saved analyses (check session or assignment).
doctorBrainMri.historySubtitleDoctor: Sessions saved under your doctor account (with or without a patient record).
doctorBrainMri.historySubtitlePatient: Analyses recorded for the patient shown.

patientBrainMri.title: Brain MRI — assisted review (your space)
patientBrainMri.subtitle: Upload a brain MRI slice for an AI-assisted screening score. Your referring physician and assigned nurse are notified when you run an analysis—this does not replace radiology or an in-person visit.
patientBrainMri.disclaimer: Decision-support tool only. Not a diagnosis. Emergency? Call emergency services. Your care team receives a notification after each analysis you run.
patientBrainMri.loginPrompt: Sign in as a patient to use this page.
patientBrainMri.careTeamNotified: Your care team (referring physician and nurse, if assigned) has been notified of this analysis.

patientAppointmentRequest.title: Appointment request
patientAppointmentRequest.intro: Enter your preferred date and time with your referring physician. Staff will confirm or suggest another slot based on availability.
patientAppointmentRequest.noDoctorWarning: No referring physician is assigned to your record. Contact administration to assign a physician before requesting an appointment.
patientAppointmentRequest.slotUnavailableNoAlternatives: No other free slots were found in the next three months on file. Ask your physician to fill the calendar (Appointment calendar menu) for that month, and choose a time that matches a calendar row (e.g. 11:00).
patientAppointmentRequest.suggestedSlotsTitle: Suggested free slots — pick one or adjust your request:
patientAppointmentRequest.successAlert: Request sent. You will be notified after validation.
patientAppointmentRequest.timeHelp: Must match a slot opened by your physician.
patientAppointmentRequest.sendFailed: Could not send. Please try again.

doctorHome.pieTitle: Appointments: this month vs last month
doctorHome.pieEmpty: Not enough data to compare two months.
doctorHome.cardApptsHint: Large figure: count for the current month. Curve: confirmed appointments per month (last 6 months).
doctorHome.cardPatientsHint: Total patients assigned to you as referring physician. (No per-month history yet — number only.)

patientQuestionnaires.title: Clinical questionnaires
patientQuestionnaires.subtitle: Assessments scheduled after your discharge (in addition to your daily check-in).
patientQuestionnaires.empty: No questionnaires to complete right now (J+3, J+7, etc. will appear on the scheduled date).

landing.heroImageAlt: Illustration: modern hospital environment and patient care
landing.aboutImageAlt: Illustration: healthcare professionals in a hospital setting
landing.heroTitle: Patient follow-up at CHU Abdelhamid Ben Badis
landing.heroLead: One platform for appointments, secure messaging, vitals, and post-discharge follow-up — aligned with a major teaching hospital open 24/7 across many medical specialties.
landing.aboutTitle: A major healthcare institution in Algeria
landing.aboutText: The Abdelhamid Ben Badis University Hospital Centre (CHU) in Constantine is a major health facility in Algeria, providing care 24 hours a day and covering many medical specialties. MediFollow supports this mission: smoother patient pathways, better team coordination, and secure communication within the hospital.
landing.featuresTitle: What MediFollow brings to the CHU
landing.f1Desc: Dashboard, daily check-in, and a clear view of your care pathway after discharge.
landing.f2Desc: Request a slot; your admin validates and it appears on the doctor’s agenda.
landing.f3Desc: Chat with your care team in a controlled, role-based environment.
landing.f4Desc: History of measurements and treatments, visible to authorized staff.
landing.f5Desc: Scheduled assessments (e.g. J+3, J+7) to support structured follow-up.
landing.f6Desc: Nurses can escalate to doctors; departments and roles stay in sync.
landing.cta2Title: Sign in with the account provided by your institution
landing.cta2Text: In Constantine, sign in with the credentials issued by the CHU. In-app data reflects your care pathway within the institution.
landing.audPText: Follow-ups, requests, questionnaires, and messages with your team.
landing.audSText: Agenda, dossiers, prescriptions context, and nurse-to-doctor escalations.
landing.audAText: Departments, appointment validation, questionnaires bank, and user management.
landing.footerBlurb: MediFollow is the digital coordination layer for CHU Abdelhamid Ben Badis, Constantine. For access or support, contact the relevant hospital service or use the details below.
landing.footerAddress: CHU Abdelhamid Ben Badis, Constantine, Algeria
landing.aboutTeaserLead: A major hospital in Constantine and a platform built for the patient journey.

aboutPage.heroLead: Learn about the university hospital’s role and how MediFollow strengthens continuity of care across services, staff, and patients.
aboutPage.chuLead: The Abdelhamid Ben Badis University Hospital Centre carries a demanding public service mission.
aboutPage.chuP1: As a major facility in Algeria, the CHU provides care 24 hours a day, covers many medical and surgical specialties, and trains future health professionals through its academic mission.
aboutPage.chuP2: Quality of the patient pathway—from admission to post-discharge follow-up—depends on coordination between units, traceability of information, and communication among care teams.
aboutPage.mfLead: MediFollow is not a scanned paper file: it is a coordination tool for the CHU.
aboutPage.mfP1: The application connects the patient space (appointments, secure messaging, questionnaires, vitals) with doctor, nurse, and admin workspaces. Requests are validated by role, nurse-to-doctor escalations are tracked, and everyone sees the right information at the right time.
aboutPage.m1Text: Reduce information gaps between stays, outpatient visits, and return home.
aboutPage.m2Text: Controlled exchanges, traceability, and role-based separation within the CHU.
aboutPage.m3Text: Give patients simple ways to stay in touch with their care team.
aboutPage.v1Text: Features aligned with real workflows in clinical and administrative departments.
aboutPage.v2Text: Emphasize cooperation between doctors, nurses, and clerical staff—not silos.
aboutPage.v3Text: Evolving pathways (e.g. J+3, J+7 questionnaires) according to CHU protocols.
aboutPage.ctaLead: Sign in with credentials issued by the CHU, or return home to explore features.

featuresPage.heroTitle: What MediFollow offers at the hospital
featuresPage.heroLead: Patient portal, appointments, secure messaging, vitals, questionnaires, and staff coordination—one foundation for follow-up and traceability.
featuresPage.introTitle: Six pillars for the care pathway
featuresPage.introLead: Each module connects with the others so patients and teams share the same information when it matters.
featuresPage.ctaLead: Sign in with credentials issued by the hospital, or return home to browse the site.

contactPage.heroLead: Questions about MediFollow, platform access, or help reaching the right department at CHU Abdelhamid Ben Badis, Constantine.
contactPage.introLead: Use the address, email, or phone below as appropriate. For urgent clinical matters, follow the hospital’s usual pathways.
contactPage.hoursText: Requests about the app and user accounts are handled by authorized hospital services during their opening hours.
contactPage.accountHint: Patient and staff accounts are issued by the hospital administration; MediFollow does not create credentials from this page.
contactPage.emergencyNote: In a medical emergency, do not rely only on in-app messaging: contact emergency services or the hospital emergency department.
contactPage.ctaLead: If you already have credentials, sign in to use MediFollow.
contactPage.formLead: Fill in the form below. Your message will be sent securely to our team by email.
contactPage.formValidation: Please fill in all fields with a valid email address.
contactPage.formSuccess: Your message has been sent successfully. We will get back to you as soon as possible.
contactPage.formErrorGeneric: Could not send your message. Please try again in a few moments.

videoMeetingPage.title: Video Meetings
videoMeetingPage.subtitle: Schedule and join secure video consultations
videoMeetingPage.emptyUpcoming: No upcoming meetings. Create one to get started!
videoMeetingPage.meetingTitlePlaceholder: e.g. Patient Follow-up with Dr. Smith
videoMeetingPage.notesPlaceholder: Add any meeting details or agenda...

newsPage.title: Global Healthcare News
newsPage.subtitle: Stay updated with the latest in healthcare, medical research, and true global health events.
newsPage.loadError: Unable to load latest healthcare news. Please try again later.

chatbot.title: Healthcare Chatbot
chatbot.subtitle: Get instant healthcare advice in English, French, and Arabic
chatbot.welcomeTitle: How can I help you today?
chatbot.welcomeText: Ask me anything about health, symptoms, nutrition, medications, exercise, or wellness. I provide evidence-based advice in your language.
chatbot.disclaimer: AI assistant — does not replace professional medical advice.
chatbot.apiError: Could not reach the AI service.
chatbot.noApiKey: Gemini API key not configured. Add VITE_GEMINI_API_KEY to your .env file to enable the chatbot.
chatbot.suggBloodPressure: How can I naturally lower my blood pressure?
chatbot.suggHeadache: What should I do for a persistent headache?
chatbot.suggFirstAid: Basic first aid for minor burns
